exemplar.title: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: How can we reconcile the relentless drive to pursue knowledge with the finite resources we have available? Discuss with reference to the natural sciences and one other area of knowledge.

strengths: Sustained and clear focus on the prescribed title throughout the essay
Effective use of concrete, real-world examples from both natural and human sciences
Coherent and logically structured argument with a clear introduction and conclusion
Insightful connection of examples to the central knowledge question of reconciling curiosity with finite resources
Demonstrates interdisciplinary awareness by drawing on multiple areas of knowledge
weaknesses: Analysis remains largely descriptive with limited depth of critical evaluation
Insufficient engagement with core TOK concepts (e.g., justification, bias, reliability) to deepen epistemic discussion
Evaluation of alternative perspectives and implications is thin or asserted rather than critically explored
Transitions between sections (especially between natural and human sciences) lack explicit epistemic linkage
Overlooks methodological limitations and counter-examples in some case studies
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The official examinerâs total mark of 9 reflects an essay that clearly satisfies the highest band descriptors in terms of sustained focus, coherence, and effective use of specific examples across disciplines. While the discussion occasionally defaults to description and would benefit from deeper critical evaluation and explicit ToK terminology, the overall quality of argumentation, interdisciplinary insight, and clarity of structure justify a near-top-band score. Assigning 9 out of 10 aligns precisely with the official mark, acknowledging both the essayâs notable strengths and its relatively minor analytic shortcomings.

annotations.comment: The final sentence introduces a new idea about âimpossible boundariesâ without clear connection to earlier analysis. A tighter synthesis directly answering how we reconcile resource limits would strengthen the conclusion.

Throughout, there is limited explicit engagement with ToK terminology (e.g., justification, bias, perspective). The student should deploy these concepts to deepen the epistemic evaluation of examples.

The claim that the HGP ârevolutionized understandingâ omits discussion of error rates or data interpretation challenges. Evaluating the reliability and limitations of genomic data would demonstrate deeper critical engagement.
The student accurately restates the prescribed title in italics, ensuring a sustained focus on the essay question. This clear framing supports alignment with the title throughout the discussion.

The opening metaphor is engaging but overly broad. The student should link imagery directly to the knowledge question to clarify how resource scarcity impacts the justification and pursuit of knowledge.

The definition of âresourcesâ conflates physical inputs and intellectual capacities without clear distinction. The student should differentiate epistemic resources (justification, perspectives) from material inputs to sharpen the analysis.

An explicit knowledge question is not clearly formulated. The student should articulate a concise KQ (e.g., âTo what extent can innovation ethically reconcile resource scarcity with our pursuit of knowledge?â) to guide the essayâs structure.

The claim that innovation and collaboration will inherently curb scarcity is asserted without critical evaluation of technological limits or unintended consequences. Consider examining cases where technology has introduced new ethical dilemmas.

The citation of uranium supply lasting 90 years (WNA, 2024) lacks discussion of underlying assumptions or potential biases in resource estimation. A critical evaluation of the sourceâs reliability would strengthen the argument.

Describing âinnumerable examplesâ risks superficial treatment. The student should introduce a counterâexample or limitation where technological advances failed to resolve scarcity to deepen evaluation.
The discussion of intellectual resources as theories and equations effectively connects ToK concepts to the title. This demonstrates insight into the nonâmaterial dimensions of resource scarcity.
The conclusion effectively reiterates the role of technology and collaboration, reinforcing the essayâs focus. This sustained linkage to the title demonstrates coherent argument structure.

The Human Genome Project example remains largely descriptive. The student should analyze ethical considerations (e.g., data privacy) and the reliability of early sequencing methods to illuminate underlying epistemic issues.
The definition of intellectual resources repeats earlier discussion. The student should consolidate similar points to improve coherence and avoid redundancy.

While introducing the Phillips Curve, the student should integrate counterarguments (e.g., stagflation) earlier and link this to the knowledge question for clearer evaluation of competing perspectives.

The essay frequently praises interdisciplinarity without acknowledging coordination challenges or epistemic conflicts. A balanced view would consider how disciplinary paradigms can clash in collaborative research.

Advocating increased funding for all disciplines overlooks ethical questions about resource allocation. The student should evaluate criteria for prioritizing research investments in contexts of finite public funds.
The transition to human sciences is abrupt. To improve coherence, explicitly link how resource types differ between natural and human sciences and why that matters epistemically.
The description of the Laspeyres Index omits its known upward bias in measuring inflation. Discussing methodological limitations would strengthen the critical depth in the human sciences section.
The assertion that technology overcomes all resource constraints ignores disparities in access to tools like MATLAB. Consider discussing how inequitable access can perpetuate knowledge gaps.
Discussing Friedman and Phelps would benefit from evaluating their assumptions about rational expectations and the normative implications of the longârun Phillips Curve model.
The Phillips Curve graph is clear, but the student should analyze anomalies (e.g., data points during the 1960s) and discuss how empirical deviations challenge theoretical models.

The concluding analysis of the Phillips Curve example is too general. The student should specify how interdisciplinary input enriched the modelâs explanatory power and addressed resource constraints.

The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
